title: M-3PO (Emtrey)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Droid |type=New Republic |image=[[File:M3PO_egtd.png]] |name=M-3PO
"Emtrey" |homeworld=[[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] |birth= |death= |creator=[[Losca]] |manufacturer=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] |line=[[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]] |model=[[M-3PO military protocol droid]] |class=[[Military protocol droid]] |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine programming]] |pronouns=new |sensor= |plating= |armament= |equipment= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] }} {{Quote|Emtrey, if I recall his introductory monologue, is supposed to know the rules, regs, and procedures of over six million military organizations past and present.|Wedge Antilles|''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''}} '''M-3PO''', also known as '''Emtreypio''' or '''Emtrey''', was a [[Gender/Legends|masculine]] [[M-3PO military protocol droid]] that served as the [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermaster]] of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. Cobbled together from parts of other [[military protocol droid]]s, he served the Rogues from their reformation in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]] through to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Because of his improvised nature, he had a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid's]] body and the head of a [[Controller droid/Legends|spaceport control droid]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|I'm going to have to talk to Luke and find out how he puts up with his 3PO unit—wait, that won't work. I don't have a sister around here to foist the droid off on.|Wedge Antilles's private complaint about Emtrey|''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''}} Emtrey was built on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] by a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] lieutenant named [[Losca]] for the purpose of procuring goods. He had special programming to assist with that purpose: a 'scrounger mode' activated when hounded about acquiring parts. He also had a strange program that disabled his normal personality and functions when told to shut up more than three times. This program essentially turned him into a 'dumb' terminal with access to Emtrey's memory banks. Both of these features were used by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] members during the time he served with them. After the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] took [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for the first time, this terminal mode was disabled. It was later revealed that the terminal mode was actually built into Emtrey by [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] in order to discover if then [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] had been successfully brain washed into an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] Agent. Emtrey was given this and other programming that made him into a very valuable tool for a spy, which would have exposed Celchu if he was a spy if Celchu used him in that manner. Emtrey was also able to provide proof that Celchu had not met with Imperial agents as [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] accused him of doing. Horn's reappearance, followed by the betrayal of Imperial mole [[Erisi Dlarit]] made Celchu's innocence obvious. The special programming was removed during the trial, and Corran Horn realized that he had been assigned to monitor Celchu when the remote mode would not work any longer After Celchu's trial, and the departure of [[Ysanne Isard]] for [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], Rogue Squadron members resigned to topple the puppet regime she had set up on that planet. Emtrey, along with all the squadron's equipment and fighters was surplussed out by someone in the military as a way of giving back door support to the former members of Rogue Squadron. Emtrey was acquired by the former Rogues, and he served in a variety of capacities with them. He was later named liaison on board the [[Valiant (Thranta-class War Cruiser)|''Valiant'']], a droid ship that survived the destruction of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Once Isard was defeated, Emtrey returned to service with the New Republic. ==Appearances== *''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Rogue Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Krytos Trap (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Bacta War (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Solo Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[I, Jedi]]'' {{Ab|I, Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' {{1stp}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{InsiderCite|74|Who's Who in Echo Base: The Unsung Rebels of the Battle of Hoth}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 29|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 29]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=M-3PO |fi=M-3PO (Emtrey) }} [[Category:Antilles's Rogues]] [[Category:Droids of the Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Category:Droids of the New Republic]] [[Category:Droids with masculine programming]] [[Category:M-3PO military protocol droids]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
